rationale: The citing paper studies plan–actual betting deviations in sequential gambles and affective forecasting, while the cited paper is a conceptual commentary on when loss aversion/endowment effects occur in valuation contexts. This creates plausible theoretical overlap (losses vs. gains/reference dependence), but the matched evidence is only a single DOI reference with no citation context or quote showing how it was used. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only, but importance is not clear enough to confidently keep.
